# Tarmi FinTrack Pro - Database Schema Documentation

This document serves as the canonical definition of the PostgreSQL schema used by the Tarmi FinTrack Pro application. It includes the Entity Relationship structure, table definitions, and the raw SQL required to initialize the database in Supabase.

## 1. Entity Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
erDiagram
    auth_users ||--|| fintrack_profiles : "has profile"
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_accounts : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_transactions : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_parties : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_assets : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_receivables : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_recurring : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_templates : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_budgets : owns

    fintrack_accounts ||--|{ fintrack_accounts : "parent of (hierarchy)"
    
    fintrack_transactions }|--|| fintrack_accounts : "debit account"
    fintrack_transactions }|--|| fintrack_accounts : "credit account"
    fintrack_transactions }|--|| fintrack_parties : "related to"

    fintrack_parties ||--|| fintrack_accounts : "linked sub-ledger"
    
    fintrack_assets ||--|| fintrack_accounts : "linked cost account"
    
    fintrack_receivables }|--|| fintrack_parties : "billed to"
    fintrack_receivables }|--|| fintrack_accounts : "revenue/expense category"
```

## 2. Table Definitions

### `fintrack_profiles`
Stores user-specific business settings used for generating invoices and reports.
*   `id` (uuid, PK): Foreign Key to `auth.users.id`. One-to-one relationship.
*   `name` (text): Business Name.
*   `address` (text): Business Address.
*   `phone` (text): Contact Phone.
*   `email` (text): Contact Email.
*   `updated_at` (timestamp): Last update timestamp.

### `fintrack_accounts`
The Chart of Accounts (General Ledger). Supports unlimited hierarchy via self-referencing `parent_id`.
*   `id` (text, PK): Unique Account ID.
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK): Owner.
*   `code` (text): Accounting Code (e.g., '1000', '1100'). Used for sorting.
*   `name` (text): Display Name.
*   `class` (text): High-level classification ('Assets', 'Liabilities', 'Equity', 'Revenue', 'Expenses').
*   `level` (text): Hierarchy depth ('class', 'group', 'gl', 'sub_ledger').
*   `parent_id` (text, FK): ID of the parent account.
*   `normal_balance` (text): Mathematical behavior ('debit', 'credit').
*   `is_posting` (boolean): If true, transactions can be recorded against this account.
*   `is_system` (boolean): If true, critical system account that cannot be deleted.

### `fintrack_transactions`
The central ledger containing all financial movements (Double Entry).
*   `id` (text, PK): Unique Transaction ID.
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK): Owner.
*   `date` (text): ISO 8601 Date string (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`).
*   `type` (text): 'income' | 'expense' | 'transfer'.
*   `amount` (numeric): The normalized amount in Base Currency (QAR).
*   `original_amount` (numeric): The raw input amount before conversion.
*   `currency` (text): 'QAR' | 'PHP'.
*   `account_id` (text, FK): The account receiving value (Debit).
*   `payment_account_id` (text, FK): The account giving value (Credit).
*   `source` (text): 'personal' | 'stable_job' | 'side_hustle'.
*   `note` (text): Description/Memo.
*   `related_party_id` (text, FK): Optional link to `fintrack_parties`.

### `fintrack_parties`
Entities (Customers, Vendors, Employees) involved in the business.
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK).
*   `name` (text).
*   `type` (text): 'customer' | 'vendor' | 'employee'.
*   `linked_account_id` (text, FK): Link to a specific sub-ledger account in `fintrack_accounts` (e.g., "AR - John Doe").

### `fintrack_receivables`
Subsidiary ledger for tracking Invoices, Bills, and Loans. Tracks lifecycle (Pending -> Paid).
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK).
*   `type` (text): 'receivable' (Asset) | 'payable' (Liability).
*   `sub_type` (text): 'invoice' | 'bill' | 'loan'.
*   `party_id` (text, FK): Link to `fintrack_parties`.
*   `target_account_id` (text, FK): The Revenue (for Invoice) or Expense (for Bill) account.
*   `amount` (numeric): Total amount due.
*   `paid_amount` (numeric): Total amount settled so far.
*   `issue_date` (text): Date of issuance.
*   `due_date` (text): Payment deadline.
*   `status` (text): 'pending' | 'paid' | 'overdue'.
*   `recurring` (jsonb): Configuration object for auto-recurrence.

### `fintrack_assets`
Fixed Assets registry for tracking depreciation.
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK).
*   `name` (text): Asset name (e.g., "MacBook Pro").
*   `value` (numeric): Current Book Value.
*   `original_value` (numeric): Purchase Price.
*   `purchase_date` (text).
*   `useful_life_years` (numeric).
*   `last_depreciation_date` (text): Timestamp of last depreciation run.
*   `linked_account_id` (text, FK): Link to the Cost Account in `fintrack_accounts`.

### `fintrack_recurring`
Rules for automated transaction generation.
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `active` (boolean).
*   `frequency` (text): 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly' | 'yearly'.
*   `next_due_date` (text).
*   `last_run_date` (text).
*   *(Includes transaction template fields: amount, account_id, etc.)*

### `fintrack_budgets`
Monthly budget limits.
*   `month_key` (text, PK): Format `YYYY-MM`.
*   `user_id` (uuid, PK).
*   `limit` (numeric): Total monthly budget.
*   `category_limits` (jsonb): Map of `account_id` -> `limit_amount`.
*   `visible_account_ids` (jsonb): Array of account IDs tracked in this budget.

## 3. Initialization SQL Script

Use the following SQL in the Supabase SQL Editor to initialize the database.

```sql
-- =================================================================
-- 0. PERMISSIONS
-- =================================================================
GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
GRANT ALL ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
GRANT ALL ON ALL FUNCTIONS IN SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
GRANT ALL ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;

ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON TABLES TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON FUNCTIONS TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON SEQUENCES TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;

-- =================================================================
-- 1. CLEANUP (Fixes "Database error creating new user")
-- =================================================================
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on_auth_user_created ON auth.users;
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.handle_new_user();

-- =================================================================
-- 2. CREATE TABLES
-- =================================================================

-- 1. PROFILES
create table if not exists public.fintrack_profiles (
  id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null primary key,
  name text,
  address text,
  phone text,
  email text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now())
);
alter table public.fintrack_profiles enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own profile" on public.fintrack_profiles for all using (auth.uid() = id);

-- 2. ACCOUNTS
create table if not exists public.fintrack_accounts (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  code text,
  name text,
  class text,
  level text,
  parent_id text,
  normal_balance text,
  is_posting boolean,
  is_system boolean,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_accounts enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own accounts" on public.fintrack_accounts for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 3. PARTIES
create table if not exists public.fintrack_parties (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  name text,
  type text,
  email text,
  phone text,
  address text,
  linked_account_id text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_parties enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own parties" on public.fintrack_parties for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 4. TRANSACTIONS
create table if not exists public.fintrack_transactions (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  date text,
  type text,
  amount numeric,
  original_amount numeric,
  currency text,
  account_id text,
  payment_account_id text,
  source text,
  note text,
  related_party_id text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_transactions enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own transactions" on public.fintrack_transactions for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 5. ASSETS
create table if not exists public.fintrack_assets (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  name text,
  value numeric,
  original_value numeric,
  currency text,
  purchase_date text,
  useful_life_years numeric,
  last_depreciation_date text,
  note text,
  linked_account_id text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_assets enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own assets" on public.fintrack_assets for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 6. RECEIVABLES
create table if not exists public.fintrack_receivables (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  type text,
  sub_type text,
  party_name text,
  party_id text,
  target_account_id text,
  amount numeric,
  paid_amount numeric default 0,
  original_amount numeric,
  currency text,
  issue_date text, 
  due_date text,
  status text,
  notes text,
  paid_date text,
  recurring jsonb,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_receivables enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own receivables" on public.fintrack_receivables for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 7. RECURRING RULES
create table if not exists public.fintrack_recurring (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  active boolean,
  type text,
  account_id text,
  amount numeric,
  currency text,
  source text,
  payment_account_id text,
  party_id text,
  note text,
  frequency text,
  next_due_date text,
  last_run_date text,
  original_amount numeric,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_recurring enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own recurring" on public.fintrack_recurring for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 8. TEMPLATES
create table if not exists public.fintrack_templates (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  name text,
  account_id text,
  amount numeric,
  currency text,
  note text,
  icon text,
  color text,
  party_id text,
  payment_account_id text,
  source text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_templates enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own templates" on public.fintrack_templates for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 9. BUDGETS
create table if not exists public.fintrack_budgets (
  month_key text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  "limit" numeric,
  category_limits jsonb,
  visible_account_ids jsonb,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (month_key, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_budgets enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own budgets" on public.fintrack_budgets for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- =================================================================
-- 3. SETUP NEW USER TRIGGER
-- =================================================================
create or replace function public.handle_new_user()
returns trigger as $$
begin
  insert into public.fintrack_profiles (id, name, email)
  values (new.id, 'My Business', new.email);
  return new;
end;
$$ language plpgsql security definer;

create trigger on_auth_user_created
  after insert on auth.users
  for each row execute procedure public.handle_new_user();

-- =================================================================
-- 4. MIGRATIONS (Column Safety Checks)
-- =================================================================
do $$
begin
  if not exists (select 1 from information_schema.columns where table_name = 'fintrack_receivables' and column_name = 'issue_date') then
    alter table public.fintrack_receivables add column issue_date text;
  end if;
end $$;

do $$
begin
  if not exists (select 1 from information_schema.columns where table_name = 'fintrack_receivables' and column_name = 'paid_amount') then
    alter table public.fintrack_receivables add column paid_amount numeric default 0;
  end if;
end $$;
```

- Intercept new user flow with a mandatory Currency Selection Modal.

# Supabase Database Setup

If you encounter **"Database error creating new user"**, **"Permission denied"**, or data disappearing after refresh (Sync Error), run this **entire** script in the Supabase SQL Editor.

This script will:
1.  **Grant Permissions**: Fixes "permission denied" errors.
2.  **Create Tables**: Sets up the database structure (including the critical `paid_amount` column and new `base_currency` field).
3.  **Run Migrations**: Adds missing columns to existing tables.

```sql
-- =================================================================
-- 0. PERMISSIONS (Fixes "permission denied" errors)
-- =================================================================
GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;

GRANT ALL ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
GRANT ALL ON ALL FUNCTIONS IN SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
GRANT ALL ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA public TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;

ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON TABLES TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON FUNCTIONS TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;
ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON SEQUENCES TO postgres, anon, authenticated, service_role;

-- =================================================================
-- 1. CLEANUP (Fixes "Database error creating new user")
-- =================================================================
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on_auth_user_created ON auth.users;
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.handle_new_user();


-- =================================================================
-- 2. CREATE TABLES
-- =================================================================

-- 1. PROFILES (Business Details)
create table if not exists public.fintrack_profiles (
  id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null primary key,
  name text,
  address text,
  phone text,
  email text,
  base_currency text, -- NEW: Stores user's primary reporting currency
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now())
);
alter table public.fintrack_profiles enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own profile" on public.fintrack_profiles for all using (auth.uid() = id);

-- 2. ACCOUNTS (Chart of Accounts)
create table if not exists public.fintrack_accounts (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  code text,
  name text,
  class text,
  level text,
  parent_id text,
  normal_balance text,
  is_posting boolean,
  is_system boolean,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);

-- 4. TRANSACTIONS (Ledger)
create table if not exists public.fintrack_transactions (

  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  date text,
  type text,
  amount numeric, -- Stores BASE currency amount (normalized)

  account_id text,
  payment_account_id text,
  source text,
  note text,
  related_party_id text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_transactions enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own transactions" on public.fintrack_transactions for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 5. ASSETS (Fixed Assets)
create table if not exists public.fintrack_assets (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  name text,
  value numeric,
  original_value numeric,
  currency text,
  purchase_date text,
  useful_life_years numeric,
  last_depreciation_date text,
  note text,
  linked_account_id text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_assets enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own assets" on public.fintrack_assets for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 6. RECEIVABLES (Invoices/Bills)
create table if not exists public.fintrack_receivables (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  type text,
  sub_type text,
  party_name text,
  party_id text,
  target_account_id text,
  amount numeric,
  paid_amount numeric default 0, -- Track partial payments
  original_amount numeric,
  currency text,
  issue_date text, 
  due_date text,
  status text,
  notes text,
  paid_date text,
  recurring jsonb,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_receivables enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own receivables" on public.fintrack_receivables for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 7. RECURRING RULES
create table if not exists public.fintrack_recurring (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  active boolean,
  type text,
  account_id text,
  amount numeric,
  currency text,
  source text,
  payment_account_id text,
  party_id text,
  note text,
  frequency text,
  next_due_date text,
  last_run_date text,
  original_amount numeric,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_recurring enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own recurring" on public.fintrack_recurring for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 8. TEMPLATES (Shortcuts)
create table if not exists public.fintrack_templates (
  id text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  name text,
  account_id text,
  amount numeric,
  currency text,
  note text,
  icon text,
  color text,
  party_id text,
  payment_account_id text,
  source text,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (id, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_templates enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own templates" on public.fintrack_templates for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 9. BUDGETS
create table if not exists public.fintrack_budgets (
  month_key text not null,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,
  "limit" numeric,
  category_limits jsonb,
  visible_account_ids jsonb,
  updated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now()),
  primary key (month_key, user_id)
);
alter table public.fintrack_budgets enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own budgets" on public.fintrack_budgets for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- 10. CURRENCY LOGS (Migration History)
create table if not exists public.fintrack_currency_logs (
  id uuid default gen_random_uuid() primary key,
  user_id uuid references auth.users on delete cascade not null,

  new_currency text,
  conversion_rate numeric,
  migrated_at timestamp with time zone default timezone('utc'::text, now())
);
alter table public.fintrack_currency_logs enable row level security;
create policy "Manage own logs" on public.fintrack_currency_logs for all using (auth.uid() = user_id);


-- =================================================================
-- 3. SETUP NEW TRIGGER
-- =================================================================
create or replace function public.handle_new_user()
returns trigger as $$
begin
  insert into public.fintrack_profiles (id, name, email)
  values (new.id, 'My Business', new.email);
  return new;
end;
$$ language plpgsql security definer;

create trigger on_auth_user_created
  after insert on auth.users
  for each row execute procedure public.handle_new_user();

-- =================================================================
-- 4. MIGRATIONS (Fixes for existing databases)
-- =================================================================

-- Add issue_date to receivables if missing
do $$
begin
  if not exists (select 1 from information_schema.columns where table_name = 'fintrack_receivables' and column_name = 'issue_date') then
    alter table public.fintrack_receivables add column issue_date text;
  end if;
end $$;

-- Add paid_amount to receivables if missing
do $$
begin
  if not exists (select 1 from information_schema.columns where table_name = 'fintrack_receivables' and column_name = 'paid_amount') then
    alter table public.fintrack_receivables add column paid_amount numeric default 0;
  end if;
end $$;

-- Add base_currency to profiles if missing
do $$
begin
  if not exists (select 1 from information_schema.columns where table_name = 'fintrack_profiles' and column_name = 'base_currency') then
    alter table public.fintrack_profiles add column base_currency text;
  end if;
end $$;
```

# Tarmi FinTrack Pro - Database Schema Documentation

This document serves as the canonical definition of the PostgreSQL schema used by the Tarmi FinTrack Pro application. It includes the Entity Relationship structure, table definitions, and the raw SQL required to initialize the database in Supabase.

## 1. Entity Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
erDiagram
    auth_users ||--|| fintrack_profiles : "has profile"
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_accounts : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_transactions : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_parties : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_assets : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_receivables : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_recurring : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_templates : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_budgets : owns
    auth_users ||--|{ fintrack_currency_logs : owns

    fintrack_accounts ||--|{ fintrack_accounts : "parent of (hierarchy)"
    
    fintrack_transactions }|--|| fintrack_accounts : "debit account"
    fintrack_transactions }|--|| fintrack_accounts : "credit account"
    fintrack_transactions }|--|| fintrack_parties : "related to"

    fintrack_parties ||--|| fintrack_accounts : "linked sub-ledger"
    
    fintrack_assets ||--|| fintrack_accounts : "linked cost account"
    
    fintrack_receivables }|--|| fintrack_parties : "billed to"
    fintrack_receivables }|--|| fintrack_accounts : "revenue/expense category"
```

## 2. Table Definitions

### `fintrack_profiles`
Stores user-specific business settings used for generating invoices and reports.
*   `id` (uuid, PK): Foreign Key to `auth.users.id`. One-to-one relationship.
*   `name` (text): Business Name.
*   `address` (text): Business Address.
*   `phone` (text): Contact Phone.
*   `email` (text): Contact Email.
*   `base_currency` (text): Default reporting currency (e.g., 'USD', 'QAR').
*   `updated_at` (timestamp): Last update timestamp.

### `fintrack_currency_logs`
Records history of base currency migration events.
*   `id` (uuid, PK).
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK).
*   `old_currency` (text).
*   `new_currency` (text).
*   `conversion_rate` (numeric).
*   `migrated_at` (timestamp).

### `fintrack_accounts`
The Chart of Accounts (General Ledger). Supports unlimited hierarchy via self-referencing `parent_id`.
*   `id` (text, PK): Unique Account ID.
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK): Owner.
*   `code` (text): Accounting Code (e.g., '1000', '1100'). Used for sorting.
*   `name` (text): Display Name.
*   `class` (text): High-level classification ('Assets', 'Liabilities', 'Equity', 'Revenue', 'Expenses').
*   `level` (text): Hierarchy depth ('class', 'group', 'gl', 'sub_ledger').
*   `parent_id` (text, FK): ID of the parent account.
*   `normal_balance` (text): Mathematical behavior ('debit', 'credit').
*   `is_posting` (boolean): If true, transactions can be recorded against this account.
*   `is_system` (boolean): If true, critical system account that cannot be deleted.

### `fintrack_transactions`
The central ledger containing all financial movements (Double Entry).
*   `id` (text, PK): Unique Transaction ID.
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK): Owner.
*   `date` (text): ISO 8601 Date string (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`).
*   `type` (text): 'income' | 'expense' | 'transfer'.
*   `amount` (numeric): The normalized amount in Base Currency.
*   `original_amount` (numeric): The raw input amount before conversion.
*   `currency` (text): The original currency code of the transaction.
*   `account_id` (text, FK): The account receiving value (Debit).
*   `payment_account_id` (text, FK): The account giving value (Credit).
*   `source` (text): 'personal' | 'stable_job' | 'side_hustle'.
*   `note` (text): Description/Memo.
*   `related_party_id` (text, FK): Optional link to `fintrack_parties`.

### `fintrack_parties`
Entities (Customers, Vendors, Employees) involved in the business.
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK).
*   `name` (text).
*   `type` (text): 'customer' | 'vendor' | 'employee'.
*   `linked_account_id` (text, FK): Link to a specific sub-ledger account in `fintrack_accounts` (e.g., "AR - John Doe").

### `fintrack_receivables`
Subsidiary ledger for tracking Invoices, Bills, and Loans. Tracks lifecycle (Pending -> Paid).
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK).
*   `type` (text): 'receivable' (Asset) | 'payable' (Liability).
*   `sub_type` (text): 'invoice' | 'bill' | 'loan'.
*   `party_id` (text, FK): Link to `fintrack_parties`.
*   `target_account_id` (text, FK): The Revenue (for Invoice) or Expense (for Bill) account.
*   `amount` (numeric): Total amount due (Base Currency).
*   `paid_amount` (numeric): Total amount settled so far (Base Currency).
*   `issue_date` (text): Date of issuance.
*   `due_date` (text): Payment deadline.
*   `status` (text): 'pending' | 'paid' | 'overdue'.
*   `recurring` (jsonb): Configuration object for auto-recurrence.

### `fintrack_assets`
Fixed Assets registry for tracking depreciation.
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `user_id` (uuid, FK).
*   `name` (text): Asset name (e.g., "MacBook Pro").
*   `value` (numeric): Current Book Value (Base Currency).
*   `original_value` (numeric): Purchase Price.
*   `purchase_date` (text).
*   `useful_life_years` (numeric).
*   `last_depreciation_date` (text): Timestamp of last depreciation run.
*   `linked_account_id` (text, FK): Link to the Cost Account in `fintrack_accounts`.

### `fintrack_recurring`
Rules for automated transaction generation.
*   `id` (text, PK).
*   `active` (boolean).
*   `frequency` (text): 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly' | 'yearly'.
*   `next_due_date` (text).
*   `last_run_date` (text).
*   *(Includes transaction template fields: amount, account_id, etc.)*

### `fintrack_budgets`
Monthly budget limits.
*   `month_key` (text, PK): Format `YYYY-MM`.
*   `user_id` (uuid, PK).
*   `limit` (numeric): Total monthly budget.
*   `category_limits` (jsonb): Map of `account_id` -> `limit_amount`.
*   `visible_account_ids` (jsonb): Array of account IDs tracked in this budget.

Based on your request to synchronize deletions between the Journal (Transactions) and the AR/AP (Receivables/Payables) modules, I have updated `context/FinanceContext.tsx`.

Here is the summary of changes:

1.  **Updated `DELETE_TRANSACTION` Reducer Logic:**
    *   Added logic to check if the deleted transaction is linked to a Receivable/Payable (via the `Ref:ID` in the note).
    *   If the transaction is an **"Accrual"** (the creation of the invoice/bill), the corresponding Receivable record is now deleted automatically.
    *   If the transaction is a **"Settlement"** (a payment), the corresponding Receivable's `paidAmount` is reduced, and its status is reverted to `pending`.

2.  **Updated `DELETE_RECEIVABLE` Reducer Logic:**
    *   Refined the logic to delete associated transactions. It now specifically looks for `Ref:${recId}` in the transaction notes to avoid accidental deletions of unrelated transactions that might contain the ID string coincidentally.

These changes ensure that:
*   Deleting an Invoice from Journal removes it from the AR list.
*   Deleting a Payment from Journal updates the "Paid" status and balance in the AR list.
*   Deleting an Invoice from the AR list removes the GL entries from the Journal.

To update the **Income Statement** structure in the Financial Reports to separate Direct Costs from Operating Expenses and calculate Gross Profit, I will modify `pages/Reports.tsx`.

### Design Specification
1.  **Logic Update**:
    *   Separate Expense accounts into two groups:
        *   **Direct Costs**: Accounts with codes starting with '5' (e.g., `51100`).
        *   **Operating Expenses**: All other Expense accounts (e.g., `60100`).
    *   Compute **Gross Profit** = `Total Revenue` - `Total Direct Costs`.
    *   Compute **Net Profit** = `Gross Profit` - `Total Operating Expenses`.

2.  **Visual Update**:
    *   Add a dedicated section for **DIRECT COSTS** (Orange theme) under Revenue.
    *   Add a highlighted summary row for **GROSS PROFIT** (Blue/Gray background) before Operating Expenses.
    *   Rename the existing "Expenses" section to "**OPERATING EXPENSES**" (Red theme).
    *   The **NET INCOME** section remains at the bottom.

3.  **Excel Export Update**:
    *   Reflect the new structure (Revenue -> Direct Costs -> Gross Profit -> OpEx -> Net Income) in the exported Excel file.

diffs.replacement:
# Financial Analysis: "Flat Maintenance" Entry

## Observation
In the Income Statement, **2,000.00** is listed under **Direct Costs** → **Flat Maintenance**.

## Why does it appear here?
The system generates the Income Statement based on how transactions are categorized at the time of entry. 

1.  **Transaction Entry**: A transaction for **2,000.00** was recorded.
2.  **Account Selection**: The account **"Flat Maintenance"** was selected as the category.
3.  **Account Configuration**: "Flat Maintenance" is configured in the Chart of Accounts as a **Direct Cost** (Expense). 
    *   *System Logic:* Accounts with codes starting with `5` are treated as Direct Costs.

Therefore, the system calculates this 2,000 as a cost of doing business, which subtracts from your Revenue to calculate Gross Profit.

## Is this correct for a "Refundable" item?
If this 2,000 is a **Refundable Deposit** (money you expect to get back), categorizing it as a Direct Cost is technically **incorrect** from an accounting perspective.

### 1. As an Expense (Current Status)
*   **Impact**: Reduces your Net Income by 2,000.
*   **Interpretation**: The business has "spent" this money, and it is gone.

### 2. As an Asset (Recommended for Deposits)
*   **Impact**: Does **not** affect Net Income. It appears on the **Balance Sheet**.
*   **Interpretation**: The business has temporarily transferred cash to a third party but retains the right to that value.
*   **How to Fix**: Edit the transaction and change the Account/Category to an **Asset** account (e.g., create a new account "Security Deposits" under Assets, or use "General Receivables").

## Conclusion
The amount appears in Flat Maintenance simply because the transaction was tagged with that specific Expense account. To reflect its "refundable" nature (keeping your Net Income higher), it should be moved to an Asset account.

I have addressed the issue where newly created "Cash & Cash Equivalents" accounts were not showing up in the "Payment via" dropdown on the Quick Transaction form.

### Analysis
The "Payment via" dropdown filters accounts by `class === 'Assets'`. The previous implementation of the **Add Account** form in `Settings.tsx` defaulted the account class to `'Expenses'` and did not provide a way to change it. This meant any new account created, even if placed under an "Assets" parent group (like Cash & Cash Equivalents), was incorrectly saved with the `Expenses` class, causing it to be hidden from asset-specific dropdowns.

### Fix
I have updated `pages/Settings.tsx` to:
1.  **Add a Class Selector**: Added a dropdown to the "Add Account" form so you can explicitly see and set the account class.
2.  **Auto-Inheritance**: Added logic to automatically switch the `Class` to match the selected `Parent` account. If you select "Cash & Cash Equivalents" as the parent, the class will now automatically switch to "Assets".
3.  **Layout Adjustment**: Updated the form layout (`md:grid-cols-7`) to accommodate the new input field cleanly.

**Note:** For any *existing* accounts that are not showing up, you will need to delete and recreate them using this new form to ensure they are tagged with the correct "Assets" class.

entries.description: Add Class selector to Chart of Accounts form and auto-inherit class from parent
